characters.name: Dick Clark
characters.description: Dick Clarkis a producer and a character inAmerican Dreams. He is portrayed byPaul D. Roberts.Dick ClarkFull NameRichard Augustus Wagstaff Clark Jr.DOBNovember 30, 1929 in Mount Vernon, New York, USADODApril 18, 2012 in Santa Monica, California, USAAMERICAN DREAMSPaul D. Roberts as Dick ClarkPortrayerPaul D. Roberts  Dick Clark had one older brother, Bradley, who was killed in World War II. At the age of 16, he got his first job in the mail room of WRUN, a radio station in Utica, New York, which was owned by his uncle and managed by his father. He worked his way up the ranks and was promoted to weatherman before becoming a radio announcer. After graduating from Syracuse University with a degree in business administration, Clark began working at several radio and television stations before landing at WFIL radio in 1952. While working at the station, Clark became a substitute host for Bob Horn's Bandstand, an afternoon program where teenagers danced to popular music, broadcast by WFIL's affiliated television station. In 1956, Horn was arrested for drunk driving, giving Clark the perfect opportunity to step in as the full-time host.

characters.name: Elvis Presley
characters.description: Elvis Presleyis a singer.Elvis PresleyFull NameElvis Aaron PresleyDOBJanuary 8, 1935 in Tupelo, Mississippi, USADODAugust 16, 1977 in Memphis, Tennessee, USAAMERICAN DREAMSAppearanceWhere the Boys AreSongViva Las Vegas  Elvis was an American singer, songwriter, musician, and actor. Regarded as one of the most significant cultural icons of the 20th century, he is often referred to as the "King of Rock and Roll" or simply "the King". He relocated to Memphis, Tennessee with his family when he was 13 years old. His music career began there in 1954, when he recorded a song with producer Sam Phillips at Sun Records. After a series of successful network television appearances and chart-topping records, he was regarded as the leading figure of rock and roll.
